sm: EU antitrust regulation enforcing interoperability, browser engine choice, and store fee caps on gatekeeper platforms.
US federal antitrust remedies banning exclusive default search agreements to open distribution channels for competitors.

l: South Korea PIPA & Credit Information Act
sm: South Korean data privacy regime enforcing explicit consent and strict residency boundaries for cross-border personal/credit data transfers.
Japan's Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications (MIC) oversight enforcing system/network isolation from foreign parent entities.

stmt: South Korea's PIPC fined Kakao Pay KRW 5.97 billion in January 2025 for transferring the personal information of 40 million users to Alipay without consent.
fx: PIPC officially reduces or overturns the KRW 5.97 billion penalty against Kakao Pay.

stmt: Japan's MIC issued administrative guidance forcing LY Corp to separate its systems from Naver Cloud following a breach of 510,000 personal records.
fx: LY Corp cancels system separation plans or maintains integrated database access for Naver employees.

stmt: The EU DMA mandates Apple to support non-WebKit browser engines on iOS and display browser default choice screens to all EU users.
fx: Apple delays browser choice screens or restricts iOS rendering exclusively to WebKit in the EU.

mech: local-first encrypted storage and peer-to-peer data sync
lev: verifiable data compliance that avoids cross-border data transfer regulations entirely
rk: latency and sync complexity compared to centralized cloud database relays

mech: packaging as PWA or distribution via alternate EU app marketplaces
lev: bypasses Apple store review guidelines and core store fee structures
rk: restricts reach to EU-only users; non-EU users remain gated by classic iOS App Store

ld: Sovereign compliance regulations (KR PIPA, JP MIC, EU DMA) fragment centralized global cloud networks, establishing data residency boundaries that isolate users regionally.
pr: Centralized hyperscaler cloud data routing is universally legal and globally uniform.
im: Canvas OS must use a federated, local-first identity and storage architecture so user context never leaves the domestic boundary.

rec: Implement modular data residency adapters in Canvas OS that enforce strictly domestic or local-only storage partitions based on the user's IP/GPS geolocation.
fx: Global data residency regulations are harmonized, legalizing unrestricted cross-border cloud transit of personal credit records.

sm: The multi-tenant, E2EE, capability-bound OS substrate governing Canvas actions.

rec: Decompose the Logic OS substrate into 11 canonical, ordered, fail-closed engines (ADR-067).
fx: Codebase archaeology proves a simpler god-syscall model is structurally sufficient for multi-tenant E2EE.
